hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The parameter 'igsh=' is entirely stripped from both the extracted destination URL and the surrounding cleaned_text line, leaving only the clean URL base.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Output each URL in the custom format 'url[<index>=<cleaned_url>]' (e.g., url[0]=https://example.com) with sequential indices starting at 0.

trigger_keywords: test cases for feature1 plain text url cleaning
show me feature1 plain text url cleaning test cases
what are the feature1 plain text url cleaning inputs and outputs
give me the feature1_plain_text_url_cleaning test file
feature1 plain text url cleaning expected output

context_pointers.actual_meaning: The decoded destination string must have any character sequences that look like URL-encoding (e.g., 'ABOUT' where a space was encoded as '%20') converted back to their literal unescaped characters, rather than keeping the '%' symbol.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_plain_text_url_cleaning.json.description: Cleans one or more URLs found inside arbitrary text while preserving surrounding non-URL text.

feature2_commerce_product_urls.json.description: Normalizes commerce product links by keeping the durable product path and removing campaign, navigation, and share parameters.

feature3_search_and_ad_redirects.json.description: Extracts the destination URL from search, advertising, and affiliate redirect links when the original tests expose a destination field or encoded path.

feature4_video_and_music_urls.json.description: Keeps the essential media identifier parameters for video, playlist, music, and short links while removing share metadata.

feature5_social_content_urls.json.description: Removes social-network share metadata while preserving the stable content identifier or allowed story fields.

feature6_generic_tracking_parameters.jso.description: Removes cross-site analytics, campaign, empty-value, and session identifier parameters from URLs while leaving unrelated fields intact.

feature7_reference_and_knowledge_urls.js.description: Cleans reference, knowledge-base, and regional site URLs by removing tested share or navigation parameters while preserving the article path or query that identifies content.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains one or more URLs within arbitrary text string
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be line-oriented stdout containing exactly three lines: 'cleaned_text=<string>', 'url_count=<number>', followed by 'url[<index>]=<cleaned_url>' lines

_constraints.input_condition: Input URL contains URL-encoded tracking fragments (e.g., igsh=YmMyMTA2M2Y="
_constraints.output_rule: The tracking fragment must be removed from both the extracted URL and the surrounding cleaned_text

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains Amazon product URLs
_constraints.output_rule: Remove all query parameters containing 'pd_rd_', 'pf_rd_', '_encoding=UTF8', or 'ref_' prefixes, keeping only the base product ID path

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains Amazon 'gp/css' homepage URLs with navigation parameters
_constraints.output_rule: Remove 'ref_' navigation parameters and return the base path

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains eBay item URLs
_constraints.output_rule: Remove all tracking parameters (mkcid, mkevt, mkrid, sspo, ssrc, ssuid, widget_ver, media) and return the base item path

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a Google search result URL
_constraints.output_rule: Extract the destination URL from the 'url' query parameter, decoding any URL-encoding within that parameter value

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a Google search result URL with destination in 'q' parameter
_constraints.output_rule: Extract the destination URL from the 'q' query parameter

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a YouTube redirect URL containing an encoded 'q' parameter
_constraints.output_rule: Decode the URL in the 'q' parameter. Note: The 'start' parameter is ignored; 'redir_token' is ignored. Preserve protocol scheme from the decoded destination.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a mobile YouTube link with share search parameters
_constraints.output_rule: Remove 'pp=' (search query) and 'start' or other transient parameters, keeping 'v=' (video ID). Handle percent-encoded search terms.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a YouTube watch URL with a timestamp parameter 't'
_constraints.output_rule: The timestamp parameter 't' must be PRESERVED in both cleaned_text and url extraction

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a YouTube playlist URL
_constraints.output_rule: Preserve the 'list' parameter. Ignore 'si' (single_item) parameter.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a Facebook Reel URL
_constraints.output_rule: Remove 'sfnsn=' (sharing network identifier) parameter

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a Facebook Story URL
_constraints.output_rule: Remove global 'sfnsn=' and 'mibextid=' parameters. PRESERVE 'story_fbid=' and 'id=' parameters.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a Twitter/X link with parameters 't' and 's'
_constraints.output_rule: Remove 't=' and 's=' parameters

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a generic URL with analytics, utm, or gclid parameters
_constraints.output_rule: Remove all query parameters

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains fb_abc, fbclid, or sfnsn parameters
_constraints.output_rule: Remove these specific tracking parameters

_constraints.input_condition: Input begins with a domain followed by a void query string (e.g., ?wt_abc=123&wt_efg=456)
_constraints.output_rule: If all remaining query parameters match generic/tracking patterns, remove all fields resulting in a base URL with no query string

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a Wikipedia page with 'wprov=' parameter
_constraints.output_rule: Remove 'wprov=' parameter

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a Yandex search URL
_constraints.output_rule: Keep 'text=' parameter. Remove 'lr=' (region) and 'clid=' parameters

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains multiple URLs (not tested but implied by feature scope)
_constraints.output_rule: Output url_count reflects total number of URLs found. Each URL gets a sequential index starting at 0. json[0], json[1], etc.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains YouTube redirect link, output must use the explicit destination URL preserved in encoded form within the link
_constraints.output_rule: Quote format: 'https://www.google.com/about/' instead of 'https://www.google.comABOUT/'

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains URL-encoded special characters (UTF-8) in product path (e.g. https://www.amazon.de/Xiaomi-Aktivit%C3%A4tstracker...)
_constraints.output_rule: UTF-8 encoding in the product path must be preserved during normalization
